rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE HORNSBY VS. NEW CENTURY MORTGAGE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For New Trial On Summary Judgment And To Vacate Judgment Entered In Favor Of Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 19, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFF ANNETTE HORNSBY's Notice Of Motion For New Trial On Summary Judgment and To Vacate Judgment Entered In Favor Of Defendant DENIED. The motion is untimely. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE HORNSBY VS. NEW CENTURY MORTGAGE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Set Aside Both Summary Judgment Order Granted In Favor Of Deft Litton Loan Servicing Lp & Deutsche Bank And Granted In Favor Of Deft First American Trustee Servicing Solution
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 19, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFF ANNETTE HORNSBY's Motion To Set Aside Both Summary Judgment Order Granted In Favor Of Defendant Litton Loan Servicing Lp & Deutsche Bank And Granted In Favor Of Defendant First American Trustee Servicing Solution DENIED. CCP 473(b) is not applicable. See English v. IKON Business Solutions (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 130. Even if it was applicable Plaintiff failed to sustain burden and show mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 1008(A) Of The Court'S January 13, 2014 Order Sustaining Defendant Sutton'S Demurrer To Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 19, 2014, line 2. PLAINTIFFs NOEL HALLETT, ABBY COOLEY, TARA HITZEMAN and HOLLI HITZEMANS' Motion For Reconsideration Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 1008(A) Of The Court'S January 13, 2014 Order Sustaining Defendant Sutton'S Demurrer To Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend GRANTED. The Court on its own motion reconsiders its January 13, 2014 Order sustaining demurrer without leave to amend. The Court now rules as follows - demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege causes of action not precluded by res judicata, i.e. not raised in small claims action. Sanders v. Walsh (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 855. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file amended complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GWYN LI JUN MIAO et al VS. MARIA T. LOPEZ et al

PLAINTIFFs GWYN MIAO and HONGXIU HE'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Obey Court Orders GRANTED. Defendants answer is hereby stricken. Plaintiff may seek entry of default. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TED FELDMAN VS. DORIS KOBUCHI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 19, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT DORIS KOBUCHI, ANNA MCCARTHY, SHIRLEY FONG and JEAN FONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT continued to April 18, 2014. Moving party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than March 31, 2014 with cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MAYFIELD et al VS. BALDWIN FRANK MYERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion 1) Removing Ashok Patel From The Office At The Property 2) Requiring Him To Turn Over The Office Keys 3) Requiring Him To Turn Over All Rents And Funds Currently In His Possession From The Property 4) Precluding Him From Interfering With Any New Management Appointed By The Owners
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 19, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT BALDWIN MYERS' Notice Of Motion 1) Removing Ashok Patel From The Office At The Property 2) Requiring Him To Turn Over The Office Keys 3) Requiring Him To Turn Over All Rents And Funds Currently In His Possession From The Property 4) Precluding Him From Interfering With Any New Management Appointed By The Owners DENIED. Opposing party and his counsel are ordered again (See CUD-13-645213 June 10, 2013 Minute Order) to comply with SFLR 2.6B (regarding courtesy copies) and in future. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES W. WELSH, TRUSTEE OF THE EXEMPTION TRUST et al VS. SACHA TUENI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 19, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANT SACHA TUENI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Conversion Ordinance prohibits offering the property for transient/tourist use. = (501/REQ)
